title: Agent With Tool Use and Approval Boundaries
description: Build an agentic workflow that can plan, call tools, log decisions, and stop for human approval at the right moment.
whyItMatters: This is one of the clearest portfolio signals for frontier product and platform roles today: useful autonomy with bounded risk.

steps.title: Define the agent's domain
steps.description: Choose a specific use case: a research agent that answers questions by searching the web and reading pages, a data analyst agent that queries databases and creates visualisations, or a DevOps agent that monitors services and takes corrective actions.

steps.title: Implement tool definitions
steps.description: Define 3-5 tools the agent can use. Each tool has a name, description, and parameter schema. Implement the actual tool functions in Python. Example tools: web_search, read_webpage, query_database, send_email, create_chart.

steps.title: Build the ReAct loop
steps.description: Implement the core agent loop: (1) Present the task and available tools to the LLM. (2) The LLM decides which tool to call. (3) Execute the tool and return the result. (4) The LLM reasons about the result and decides the next action. (5) Repeat until the task is complete or a limit is reached.

steps.title: Add safety guardrails
steps.description: Implement safety measures: maximum number of tool calls per task (prevent infinite loops), confirmation before destructive actions (deleting data, sending emails), input validation for tool parameters, and output filtering for sensitive information.

steps.title: Implement error handling
steps.description: Tools fail. APIs time out. The LLM generates invalid tool calls. Handle all of these gracefully: retry with backoff, inform the agent of the error so it can try a different approach, and fail gracefully with a clear error message if recovery is impossible.

steps.title: Add tracing and observability
steps.description: Log every step of the agent's reasoning: the thought, the tool call, the tool result, and the next decision. This trace is essential for debugging, evaluating, and improving the agent.

steps.title: Evaluate on test scenarios
steps.description: Create 10-15 test scenarios with expected outcomes. Run the agent on each and evaluate: did it complete the task? How many steps did it take? Did it use the right tools? Did it handle errors correctly?

rubric.description: The agent successfully completes at least 70% of test scenarios using appropriate tools.
Agent reasoning traces show logical step-by-step thinking. Tool choices are appropriate for the task.
Guardrails prevent infinite loops, destructive unchecked actions, and information leakage.
The agent handles tool failures gracefully — retries, tries alternative approaches, or fails with clear explanations.
